segment-text: a screenplay by John R. Stevenson

WE SEE a girl standing in about a foot of snow. She faces the woods. Her face is in awe. She pans the woods up and down with her eyes. She steadily starts walking further and further...and starts gaining a faster pace as the epic-ness begins. Once she's in the woods, it grows very dark. She looks back for a moment...then back around to be struck in the face by a bright light. It fades and she can see a man standing on a small hill. She walks to him.
Mom and Dad want you to come home now.
The boy starts to quiver. Tracy grows scared. Blood begins to run from his mouth.
A chain saw rips through Jason's chest. Tracy screams! WE PAN BACK to reveal a masked man standing behind him, holding the machine. Tracy still screams as her brother is being ripped up.
WE SEE Tracy lying in bed...eyes wide open. She's breathing heavy. She looks around, and then sits up. She sees her roommate staring at her.
Sophie drops her head...so does Tracy.
WEE SEE sign that reads MAIN LODGE AND DINING. Tracy and Sophie run past it in a hurry.
We are going to be so late.
Tracy gets up on the porch and gives her boyfriend, MIKE, a hug. The head counselor walks out, followed by another guy.
Alright everybody! Listen up. We got a lot of work ahead of us over the next few days so lock this place down for the winter.
Two of the counselors hug each other.
As I was saying. We got a lot of work over the next few days so we can lock this place down. So I arranged for an extra set of hands.
He puts his hand on a guy's shoulder.
So I want to you meet James. He's an old family friend.
James is hitching his way to USC. He's a got full scholarship no less.
I roped him into sticking around for a couple days, helping us. Let's get to it. Any volunteers who want to pair up with James for the day?
Okay. Nice to see your enthusiasm, Sophie. Oh, one more thing. We got some weather coming in so if you want to grab some extra blankets from the shed tonight, go ahead.
Alright. Let's go everyone. Let's go.
Everyone walks away except Ryan and Angela...they're kissing.
Ryan! Can I see you for a moment? Did you put the wood chipper back in the shed like I asked you to last night?
I'll take care of it right now.
No, it's too late. The rain last night ruined the motor.
I'm sorry. It won't happen again.
It's just like the leaf blower.
The leaf blower wasn't my fault.
Look! Why are you always on my ass?
You heard me. Look, I'm not impressed by your macho bull shit like your little girlfriend. I'm going to say this one: your screw ups are costing me a lot money and I don't like it.
So what are you going to do, Rick? Are you going to fire me, with only three days left?
We see Mike and Tracy walking to us. Tracy is carrying blankets.
Ah. The trees are turning early this year.
Yeah. So much for our Indian summer, huh?
Yeah. It's kind of lonely here without all of the kids.
Do not tell me you miss them.
It's sad. The end of summer and everything.
The two help each other spread the blanket over a close line.
Now that you bring it up. (pointing) Nah.
Come on, mister tough guy. Now you can't fool me. Inside you're just a geat big softy.
Actually, you know that kid with the lazy eye? He kind of got to me a little bit.
I'd like to propose a toast.
May we not have to clean up another camper's puke for another ten months.
And now that the little bastards have gone home to their mommies and daddies, we can turn our thought to more adult pursuits.
How about a game...of (cringes) Bloody Murder?
Great idea. Who's up for it? Elvis...
Don't be a chicken shit, you're it.
How about you Mike? You up for it?
Actually. I think I'm going to pass.
It's fine. You guys have fun.
Actually I'm really tired. I'm just going to go to bed early.
Not tonight, I'm serious. You guys have fun, okay?
Jesus. You guys are so insensitive.
Don't play innocent. You know exactly what you were doing. Mike of all people should know better.
I think I might have missed something.
Tracy's brother Jason disappeared up here about five years ago.
Well, there was a series of murders that summer, and Jason's body was never found. The locals claim it was Trevor Moorehouse.
We see Mike and Tracy walk up to Tracy's cabin.
I'm sorry, Tracy, that was--that was insensitive of me.
It's okay. I didn't even mean to make a thing out of it.
Why don't you and I hang here while the others run around the woods like idiots?
I'm sensing you're trying to tell me no.
I've tried really hard to get close to you this summer.
Don't make a thing out of it.
I'm just saying I've tried so hard to be sensitive to your family situation.
My family situation? Is that really what you think of this?
I didn't mean it to sound like that.
I can't get close to you. You put up this front.
I'm tired of being treated like I've got six heads.
Then you shouldn't have told us about your brother.
I'm just saying it's a little confusing. You know, you either want the attention or you don't.
I don't think I want to talk about this anymore.
She leaves and walks inside the cabin.
Mike walks up to the fire where everyone else is.
Looks like somebody's not getting any tonight.
Yeah. What else is new, man?
James has been asking about Trevor Moorehouse.
Fuck him. That guy's putting a serious dent in my sex life.

Trevor was the son of the meanest camp director that Placid Pines has ever seen. He had this habit of ratting on the counselors to his old man. Well, one year...several of the counselors got this half-assed plan to get back at them. But things got out of control, and before they knew it, Trevor was at the bottom of a ravine half dead with his face eaten by hungry birds.
Naturally, he went to the hospital. Medical at first, then mental. His face was so badly disfigured that he always wore a mask.

Every summer camp has a Trevor Moorehouse. Here, let me see. At some point he was either released, or he broke out of the mental hospital. Then it's said that he returned to Camp Placid Pines to forever seek out his revenge on the place and the people...that did this to him. How'd I do?
Set up the new guy, huh? Nice try, boys. Trevor Moorehouse is just some fictitious local psychopath to get campers into bed at night.
Did you really think I'd be stupid enough to fall for that?
You think I'm making it up?
I hope not. I would think you'd be able to come up with something a little better than the old "psychopath in the woods" story.
Well then, tough guy. Since you've got nothing to fear you won't mind being "It."
Okay sports fans. It's time for a little game of --
Angela jumps up to Ryan, hugging.
She walks over to James with it.
Wait a minute. I didn't say anything --
What's the matter, Jimbo? Huh? You having a little change of heart?
You afraid Trevor might break up the game?
Good! Then let the pretty lady blindfold you. Remember...there's nothing to fear.
The group walks to a big tree in the middle of the woods. James is blindfolded.
You've got to count to 20 before you can remove the blindfold.
The group runs off to hide.
One-one thousand. Two-one thousand. Three-one thousand. Four-one thousand. Five-one thousand...eighteen-one thousand. Nineteen-one thousand. Twenty-one
He turns around and removes the blindfold. He looks around. Nothing.
Oh, man. Me and my big mouth.
James walks off to search for the rest of the group. Each of the people are hiding in a different location. James backs up into a tree and gasps.
(peeking through trees) You're not in Compton anymore.
James sees Mike gagging by a tree, back to him. Mike turns around. His neck is bloody as he holds it. He spits up blood. James begins to back up.
James turns around in horror. There's a hockey-masked figure with an axe coming up behind James.
He screams as the masked man yells with the axe in the air. The yelling turns into laughter. Mike is okay, and the man removes his mask. It is Ryan.
(laughing) You were saying something about fictitious local psychopaths?
We're just having a little fun at James' expense.
Tell him about the psychopath, then they come out dressed -- that's hot, baby.
That was beautiful with the blood.
Thank you. Thank you very much.
(pretend crying) Trevor Moorehouse! Oh my God!
Ryan tackles James to the ground. Ryan falls first though.
You're dead, you little fucker! Don't fuck with me! Fuck you, bitch.
(pointing) Get your little friend out of here before he gets hurt.
They all runs back to the cabin except for James and Sophie.
Yeah. He's not the first asshole I've had to deal with. He certainly won't be the last.
I'm gonna head back up, but I'll see you in the morning.
Yeah, if I decide to stay.
Don't let that loser get to you.
Sophie walks back to the camp.
